_help._note: Allowed values + requirements per field (this _help block is ignored by the gate — keep or delete). Run `plumb schema` for the full reference.
_help.task_id: required — ticket/issue/branch id (also the receipt filename)
_help.agent_id: required — which agent or human did the work
_help.intent: required — what + why, plain language — the semantic review reads this
_help.self_modifying: one of: true | false — required — MUST be true if changed_files touch policy.protected_paths; touching one with false is a hard fail; true routes to human review
_help.validation_plan: required — each: { command, reason, required, id?, ci_covered? }
_help.validation_plan[].required: one of: true | false — required — is this check mandatory
_help.validation_plan[].id: optional — optional step id; evidence is matched to it via execution_evidence[].step (robust to a command wording diff)
_help.validation_plan[].ci_covered: one of: true | false — optional — step is corroborated by the ci-evidence gate (real CI run), not manual evidence — may be 'skipped'; also auto-recognized when command matches a policy ci_evidence_checks entry
_help.execution_evidence: required — each: { command, status, output_ref?, skip_reason?, step? }
_help.execution_evidence[].status: one of: passed | failed | skipped — required — required steps must be 'passed' (unless CI-covered); use skip_reason when 'skipped'
_help.execution_evidence[].step: optional — optional id of the validation_plan step this evidence is for (matches validation_plan[].id)
_help.changed_files: required — set by `plumb receipt --write` — don't hand-edit
_help.diff_sha256: required — set by `plumb receipt --write` — never hand-edit
_help.result_summary: required — what changed + how it was verified

intent: Harden the gate against the REVIEW findings its own self-hosting run raised on the just-merged #31 (provider + cost controls) and #32 (batteries-included init + setup-protection). Highest severity first: (1) setup-protection must not clobber existing branch protection — it now GETs current protection and PRESERVES required_pull_request_reviews + restrictions (only ADDS the required checks / auto-merge), and refuses to write when it can't read current protection unless --force; (2) the OpenAI-compatible provider must not fall back to ANTHROPIC_API_KEY (credential-leak to a third-party endpoint) — it now requires an explicit PLUMBLINE_API_KEY and errors clearly if missing; (3) temperature is omitted by default so Anthropic models that reject an explicit temperature don't break the gate; (4) a redundant self_modifying/protected-path floor runs in cli.ts before an approve on the review-skip path; (5) a cached verdict is only served after re-confirming receipt.diff_sha256 matches the real diff; (6) poll-wait self-detection keys on the workflow run id instead of a name substring so a renamed job or another 'plumbline'-named check can't deadlock it. Plus the missing failure-mode tests the review named. The github.ts headers->headers2 diff-integrity flag was investigated and found to be a pure esbuild bundling artifact (source github.ts was not modified in #32) — documented in the PR body.

validation_plan.reason: Type-check every changed module — the reworked protection.ts (preserve logic + normalizeForPut), provider.ts/review.ts (optional temperature, no Anthropic-key fallback), cli.ts (protectedFloorHit + cache validation), and the extended Policy type (review_temperature now optional).
tsc must still emit dist/cli.js (the npm bin) and dist/test/ — the hardening must not break the package build.

Full suite (policy required_check) incl. the new failure-mode tests: setup-protection preserves reviewers + refuses/forces on unreadable protection + strict downgrade; openai never reads/leaks the Anthropic key + missing-key error + unknown-provider error; temperature omitted-by-default and PLUMBLINE_TEMPERATURE override; protectedFloor floor from the actual diff; PLUMBLINE_MODEL/PROOFGATE_MODEL override; budget soft-cap parse; isOwnGateRun run-id self-detection; runMigrationGuard CLI path.

validation_plan.command: npm run bundle && git diff --quiet -- dist/index.js
validation_plan.reason: The committed action bundle must be byte-identical to a fresh rebuild — the dist-check CI job enforces this, and the Action runs the prebuilt dist/index.js directly.

execution_evidence.output_ref: tsc exit 0; dist/cli.js and dist/test/ emitted
184 tests, 184 pass, 0 fail (was 145 baseline; 39 new/hardening tests added)

execution_evidence.command: npm run bundle && git diff --quiet -- dist/index.js
execution_evidence.output_ref: esbuild rebuilt dist/index.js (231.4kb); committed bundle matches a fresh rebuild (git diff clean)

result_summary: protection.ts: setupProtection now GETs the current branch protection, carries required_pull_request_reviews + restrictions + enforce_admins back into the PUT via a new normalizeForPut() (down-projects the GET's expanded users/teams/apps to the login/slug arrays the PUT accepts), and refuses to write when protection is unreadable (non-200/404) unless opts.force; CLI wires --force + updated help; AGENTS.md/README/CHANGELOG document exactly what it changes and preserves. provider.ts: OpenAICompatibleProvider selection no longer reads ANTHROPIC_API_KEY (requires PLUMBLINE_API_KEY/PROOFGATE_API_KEY, error names the leak risk); CompletionRequest.temperature is optional and both providers omit it from the body when undefined. review.ts: resolveReviewTemperature (PLUMBLINE_TEMPERATURE > policy > omit); types.ts review_temperature now optional; templates/policy.json drops the hardcoded 0. cli.ts: protectedFloorHit re-checks the self_modifying/protected floor from the ACTUAL git diff before any approve on the review-skip path; cache read/write is gated on an independent recompute of diff_sha256 matching the real diff. templates/workflow.yml + github.ts isOwnGateRun: poll-wait self-detection keys on context.runId embedded in the check-run details_url (name-substring only as a last-resort fallback, timeout still bounds it). Verified by tsc --noEmit, npm run build, and 184/184 tests (39 new: protection preserve/force/strict-downgrade, provider key-leak/missing-key/unknown-provider, temperature omit+override, protectedFloor, model override, budget cap, isOwnGateRun, runMigrationGuard). github.ts headers->headers2 investigated: pure esbuild artifact (esbuild renames github.ts/ci.ts local `const headers` to avoid colliding with protection.ts's hoisted top-level `function headers` in the merged bundle scope); src/github.ts was last modified in #21, not #32 — no source change to reconcile.
